_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging global prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with 2015 as the base year at 100, currently stands at 132, reflecting a 32% increase since 2015. This indicates a substantial rise over the period, though the increase has not been uniform across all components or years. The overall climb in the index is largely driven by recent price movements in some commodities rather than a steady trend. Such an index provides a snapshot of the cost changes for a typical breakfast-related commodity basket.
notes.movers: Among the components, coffee prices have surged by 93%, making it the largest contributor to the basket's increase. Orange juice prices have also risen notably by 22%, while wheat and sugar have seen modest increases of 7% and 5% respectively. These shifts are not reflective of broad inflationary pressures but instead stem from weather events and crop diseases, such as frosts and droughts in coffee-growing regions and citrus greening affecting orange juice. Soft commodities like these are traded in relatively thin markets, so their prices can be particularly sensitive to supply disruptions.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is primarily driven by a few key crops vulnerable to weather and disease, underscoring the sector's sensitivity to environmental risks. Because these commodities are exposed to such shocks, their input costs can remain elevated for extended periods rather than adjusting quickly. This suggests that supply-side factors, rather than generalized inflation, are the main influences behind recent price developments.
